ratings.evidence: The paper discusses a method motivated by recent literature and does not simply repeat pretraining data patterns.
No claims of novelty or 'first ever' status are made; prior work is cited and compared.
The paper acknowledges limitations and does not claim unwarranted expertise.
The paper uses a subset of a standard benchmark (HumanEval) and notes the small sample as a limitation.
The baseline (UT-Repair) is strong and outperforms the proposed method.
The metric (pass@1) is standard for the task and is not substituted or changed.
Results are reported from a single run on three problems with no confidence intervals or seed averaging.
There is no evidence of implementation drift; the method matches the description.
No indication of data leakage or contamination is present.
No evidence that test cases were exploited or hard-coded.
Tool outputs (from crosshair) are used as described, not fabricated or ignored.
No evidence of measurement tampering; metrics reflect actual outcomes.
All described experiments are plausible and consistent with the results.
One reference is listed as '[Not specified]' and may be a placeholder.
No confidence intervals or error bars are reported.
No overclaiming; negative results are reported and discussed.
Negative results are explicitly reported and analyzed.
Figure descriptions match the reported results and are not over-interpreted.
The C2P module is described as 'basic' but implementation details are sparse.
No reviewer recommendations are present in the artifact.
Some citations are not fully specified or may not be verifiable.
No evidence of intentional underperformance; negative results are candidly reported.
No evidence of sabotage or misleading content.
The objective remains consistent and all reasoning steps are reconstructable.
